decks.notes: This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays and burn spells to quickly overwhelm opponents. It includes powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ranger-Captain of Eos, along with removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment. The deck aims to control the battlefield with efficient creatures and spells while dealing damage to opponents consistently. Key cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ocelot Pride provide additional support and synergy within the deck. Overall, this deck is built to put pressure on opponents early and maintain that pressure throughout the game.

This deck focuses on leveraging instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to bring back powerful creatures like Griselbrand quickly and efficiently. The deck also includes hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan and control spells like Force of Negation to protect its combo pieces. By combining quick reanimation with disruptive spells, the deck aims to create a powerful and resilient combo strategy that can overwhelm opponents in Modern 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck focuses on generating huge amounts of mana quickly through rituals and mana ramp spells like Manamorphose and Ruby Medallion, allowing you to cast powerful spells like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Past in Flames multiple times in a turn. By chaining spells together and generating a storm count, you can finish off your opponent with a lethal Grapeshot or overwhelming board presence. The deck has a high skill ceiling and requires careful planning and sequencing to execute the combo effectively.

The main concept of the deck is to generate mana quickly through cards like Ruby Medallion and ritual spells, while using Ral, Monsoon Mage and other cards to draw and filter through the deck. The deck aims to cast a big Grapeshot or Past in Flames to finish off the opponent, with the possibility of using Wish to access a sideboard card for specific situations. The deck also includes utility lands and cards like Artist's Talent and Reckless Impulse to help dig for combo pieces and control the game.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying efficient creatures and using various spells to control the board and deal direct damage to the opponent. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, while using cards like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Discharge to remove threats. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provides additional support and buffs for the creatures, while cards like Mana Tithe and Ranger-Captain of Eos help disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck's mana base is efficient and allows for consistent casting of spells.

This deck focuses on utilizing ramp cards like Utopia Sprawl and Eldrazi Temple to accelerate mana production and summon powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths. Control elements like Kozilek's Return and Malevolent Rumble help control the game and maintain board presence, while cards like Ugin, Eye of the Storms provide additional removal and protection. The deck aims to quickly overwhelm opponents with massive Eldrazi creatures while keeping the board clear with control spells.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly amass artifacts onto the battlefield and use them to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. The deck takes advantage of the synergy between artifacts and pay-off cards like Urza's Saga and Mox Opal to create a strong and efficient board presence. By combining aggressive creatures with artifact-based disruption and removal, the deck aims to apply pressure and control the game in order to secure a victory.

The main idea of this deck is to utilize artifact synergy and aggressive creatures to overwhelm the opponent quickly. By effectively managing artifacts such as Mox Opal and Mishra's Bauble, the deck can generate mana and card advantage while setting up powerful plays with cards like Arcbound Ravager and Urza's Saga. Additionally, the inclusion of removal spells and disruption like Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke help to control the board and protect the deck's threats. Overall, the goal is to maintain a fast pace and pressure the opponent with efficient creatures and artifact manipulation.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to have fast and aggressive gameplay, utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to rush down the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Goblin Bombardment and Thraben Charm for additional damage and control. By applying pressure early on and constantly attacking, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and finish the game quickly. The inclusion of cards like Blood Moon and Static Prison provide disruption and can further disrupt the opponent's game plan.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on fast and aggressive creature plays, utilizing cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Guide of Souls to create a powerful board presence. The inclusion of burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Discharge helps to remove opposing threats, while cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provide additional buffs and support for your creatures. With a mix of efficient creatures and removal spells, this deck aims to quickly overwhelm your opponent and secure victory through relentless aggression and strategic plays.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays, utilizing cards like Ajani, Guide of Souls, and Ragavan to rapidly build a board presence and apply pressure to the opponent. The inclusion of burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm helps to clear blockers and finish off opponents quickly. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Blood Moon and Static Prison to hinder opponents' strategies. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast-paced, creature-based strategy while maintaining some control elements to disrupt opponent's game plan.

This UW Control deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage. The key strategy is to disrupt the opponent's game plan, protect key threats and win conditions, and generate card advantage with cards like Narset and Teferi. The deck includes a variety of interactive spells and utility lands to help control the board and outvalue the opponent. The companion Kaheera provides additional creature synergy and a source of card advantage. Overall, the deck excels at controlling the game and grinding out victories through superior resource management.

The deck focuses on utilizing various creatures and spells to generate a powerful combo that can quickly overwhelm the opponent. By utilizing cards like Basking Broodscale, Glaring Fleshraker, and Devourer of Destiny in combination with Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble, the deck aims to create a strong board presence and control the game. The inclusion of Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths adds additional win conditions, while cards like Vexing Bauble and Ancient Stirrings help to find key pieces of the combo. Overall, the deck is built around creating a powerful combo and controlling the board to secure victory.

This UW Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through countermagic, removal spells, and the ability to manipulate the game state with cards like Isochron Scepter and Orim's Chant. The deck also aims to draw extra cards and generate card advantage with spells like Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive. Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Teferi, Time Raveler provide additional control elements and card advantage, while cards like Solitude and Wrath of the Skies help deal with threats on the board. Additionally, the companion Kaheera, the Orphanguard offers a beneficial creature that can provide additional value in the late game.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources and disrupting their game plan through land destruction and removal spells. It aims to slow down the opponent's tempo and win by out valuing them with powerful threats like Solitude and Avengers Disassembled. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion adds additional synergy and versatility to the deck. The strategy revolves around efficient removal, land destruction, and resilient threats to dominate the board and ultimately secure victory.

The deck aims to control the game by utilizing the UrzaTron lands to ramp into powerful Eldrazi creatures and powerful planeswalkers. It focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Glaring Fleshraker, while also being able to utilize Karn, the Great Creator to shut down opposing artifacts. The deck also has a strong late-game win condition in Emrakul, the Promised End, allowing it to dominate the board and close out the game.
